verbGlossary.ACCOMPANIES: {source} is bundled with {target} submission as a required attachment (e.g., drawing set accompanies building permit application).
verbGlossary.ADDRESSES REQUIREMENTS OF: {source} content directly responds to {target} code requirements (e.g., specification section addresses health department plumbing code requirements).
verbGlossary.AMENDS: {source} formally modifies a previously filed permit application or submission (e.g., addendum amends {target} application package after bid-phase changes).
verbGlossary.ATTACHES TO: {source} is appended as supporting evidence to a permit filing (e.g., certificate of insurance attaches to {target} application as proof of coverage).
verbGlossary.BACKS: {source} provides contractual backing or guarantee referenced by a regulatory {target} (e.g., warranty documentation backs the letter of compliance).
verbGlossary.CAPTURES PLACEMENT FOR: {source}record shows material or reinforcement positioning required by a special inspection (e.g., photo captures rebar placement for structural special inspection).
verbGlossary.CERTIFIES MATERIALS FOR: {source} provides manufacturer certification that materials meet regulatory standards (e.g., product data certifies fixture materials for health department approval).
verbGlossary.CLARIFIES COMMENTS FROM: {source} seeks clarification on review comments issued by a regulatory plan reviewer (e.g., RFI clarifies building department plan review redline comments).
verbGlossary.CLEARS DEFICIENCIES FOR: {source}document demonstrates all outstanding items are resolved, enabling {target} issuance (e.g., completed punch list clears deficiencies for certificate of occupancy).
verbGlossary.CLEARS ENCUMBRANCE FOR: {source}release document removes a legal hold that would otherwise block a regulatory {target} (e.g., lien waiver clears encumbrance for certificate of completion).
verbGlossary.CONFIRMS APPROVAL BY: {source} response documents that a regulatory reviewer has accepted the submission (e.g., submittal response confirms approval by the building department plan reviewer).
verbGlossary.CONTRIBUTES TO: {source} feeds into the overall completeness score of a regulatory compliance measure (e.g., as-built documentation contributes to compliance document completeness).
verbGlossary.COORDINATES: {source} or communication aligns schedules between the project team and a regulatory process (e.g., OAC meeting coordinates inspection scheduling with {target}).
verbGlossary.DETAILS LAYOUT FOR: {source} provides precise spatial arrangement required by a regulatory reviewer (e.g., shop drawing details fire sprinkler layout for {target} review).
verbGlossary.DOCUMENTS CONDITIONS FOR: {source}record captures site conditions relevant to {target} (e.g., photo documents exposed framing conditions for AHJ inspection).
verbGlossary.DOCUMENTS IMPACT OF: {source} formally records the schedule or cost consequences of a regulatory action (e.g., notice of delay documents impact of a regulatory hold on the project).
verbGlossary.EVIDENCES COMPLIANCE FOR: {source} demonstrates that proposed materials or methods meet {target} requirements (e.g., submittal evidences code compliance for trade permit issuance).
verbGlossary.FILES FOR: {source} formally initiates the application process for a specific {target} type (e.g., permit application document files for a demolition permit).
verbGlossary.FORMALIZES: {source} converts a {target} into a binding project-specific plan (e.g., safety plan document formalizes the site safety plan requirement).
verbGlossary.FULFILLS: {source} satisfies the full regulatory requirement of {target} (e.g., SWPPP document fulfills the environmental permit requirement).
verbGlossary.FURNISHES SPECS FOR: {source} provides technical specifications needed by an independent testing or inspection entity (e.g., product data furnishes specs for third-party testing).
verbGlossary.IMPLEMENTS: {source}plan into day-to-day practice on site (e.g., site instruction implements site safety plan protocols for a specific work activity).
verbGlossary.INITIATES: {source} formally starts {target} lifecycle process (e.g., permit application document initiates the permit application workflow).
verbGlossary.LOGS OUTCOME OF: {source}records the result of {target} event (e.g., daily report logs the outcome of an AHJ inspection visit).
verbGlossary.MODIFIES SCOPE OF: {source} changes the permitted scope of work, potentially requiring {target} revision (e.g., ASI modifies scope covered by the building permit).
verbGlossary.PACKAGES: {source} organizes and delivers a set of documents for a regulatory submission (e.g., transmittal packages {target} application documents for submission to the AHJ).
verbGlossary.PROVIDES MIX DESIGN FOR: {source} furnishes concrete or material mix proportions required by a special inspection (e.g., submittal provides concrete mix design for structural special inspection).
verbGlossary.PROVIDES SPECIMENS FOR: {source} submittal delivers physical material specimens required for special inspection testing (e.g., sample submittal provides mortar specimens for masonry special inspection).
verbGlossary.RECORDS INCIDENT FOR: {source}captures a safety or environmental event that may trigger a regulatory notification (e.g., daily log note records a safety incident for OSHA notification).
verbGlossary.RECORDS STATUS OF: {source} capture the current state of a permit or regulatory process (e.g., meeting minutes record {target} status discussed at the OAC meeting).
verbGlossary.RECORDS TESTING FOR: {source}captures the execution of a test procedure required by a third-party inspector (e.g., video records load testing for third-party structural testing).
verbGlossary.REFERENCES CODE FOR: {source} cites specific {target} sections applicable to a regulatory review (e.g., specification section references fire code for building department plan review).
verbGlossary.REMEDIATES FAILURES FROM: {source} prescribes corrective measures to address deficiencies found during {target} (e.g., action plan remediates failures identified during an AHJ inspection).
verbGlossary.REQUESTS DETERMINATION FROM: {source} asks {target} to issue a formal ruling or interpretation (e.g., correspondence requests a zoning determination from the planning department).
verbGlossary.REQUIRES REVISION TO: Plan change triggers the need to amend an already-issued {target} (e.g., plan revision notice requires revision to the building permit to reflect design changes).
verbGlossary.RESOLVES COMMENTS FROM: {source} addresses open review comments from a regulatory reviewer (e.g., RFI resolves {target} review comments on the egress plan).
verbGlossary.RESUBMITTED TO: {source}document is sent back to {target} for re-review after initial comments (e.g., drawing revision is resubmitted to building department plan review).
verbGlossary.ROUTED TO: {source} is directed to a specific regulatory reviewer for discipline-specific review (e.g., drawing set is routed to {target} for fire and life safety review).
verbGlossary.SATISFIES: {source} meets the full {target} of a specific permit (e.g., SWPPP document satisfies the stormwater permit requirement).
verbGlossary.SATISFIES CORRECTIONS FOR: {source} resolves correction items noted during {target} (e.g., RFI response satisfies corrections required after an AHJ inspection).
verbGlossary.SCHEDULES REINSPECTION VIA: Task line item arranges a follow-up {target} after corrective work (e.g., action plan task schedules reinspection via the inspection scheduling process).
verbGlossary.SCOPES WORK REQUIRING: {source}defines the extent of work that will need a specific {target} type (e.g., RFP scopes mechanical work requiring a trade permit).
verbGlossary.STANDARDIZES: {source}establishes a consistent format for a regulatory process (e.g., proforma form standardizes {target} application submission format).
verbGlossary.SUBMITTED TO: {source} is formally delivered to {target} for official review (e.g., drawing set is submitted to the building department for plan review).
verbGlossary.SUBSTANTIATES: {source} provides the evidentiary basis for issuing a regulatory {target} (e.g., as-built documentation substantiates the certificate of completion).
verbGlossary.SUPPORTS FILING OF: {source} provides required background material for a regulatory notification (e.g., safety plan supports the filing of an OSHA notification).
verbGlossary.SUPERSEDES PLANS FOR: {source}approved plans referenced by {target} (e.g., drawing revision supersedes the approved plans used during an AHJ inspection).
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS AMENDMENT TO: {source}required modification to an existing {target} (e.g., change directive triggers amendment to a renovation/alteration permit).
verbGlossary.DELIVERS TURNOVER FOR: {source}document delivers required operational information as a condition of {target} (e.g., O&M manual delivers turnover documentation for certificate of occupancy).
verbGlossary.VERIFIES COVERAGE FOR: {source}document confirms required coverage levels for a labor or safety compliance requirement (e.g., COI verifies workers' comp coverage for labor compliance).
